page: Amazon Web Services t2.small is a burstable performance instance belonging to the t2 family, utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It features a single physical core and single vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, based on the i386 architecture. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the t2.small in the bottom 10% across almost all evaluated categories. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU benchmarks both score 855.28, reflecting the single-threaded nature of the resource. Memory bandwidth is limited, with a read-only small block speed of 78.28 GB/sec and a large block read speed of 9.8 GB/sec. Cryptographic and compression tasks also rank in the lower tier, with a passmark CPU encryption score of 482.13 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 19.7 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation and low resource density, the t2.small is positioned as a budget-friendly option for low-intensity workloads. It is suitable for basic tasks such as light static web serving, which registers a throughput of 1.09 GB/sec, or simple database operations. It is not designed for continuous high-CPU workloads, data science, or large-scale applications, but serves as an entry-level environment for development, testing, or microservices that remain idle most of the time.
description: Amazon Web Services t2.small is a burstable, entry-level instance designed for low-intensity workloads. Running on the Xen hypervisor with an i386 architecture, it features 1 vCPU from an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of memory. The instance lacks local storage, dedicated GPUs, and baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results consistently rank the t2.small in the bottom 10% for CPU, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations, with a stress-ng score of 855.28 and a static web serving throughput of 1.09 GB/sec. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for development, testing, and microservices with low CPU utilization requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t2.small is a burstable instance featuring 1 vCPU, an Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor, and 2.0 GB of memory, designed for low-intensity workloads and budget-sensitive development.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t2.small is a burstable i386 instance with 1 vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, built for low-demand workloads.
tagline: A burstable, entry-level compute instance with minimal resource requirements, designed for low-intensity development and testing workloads.
bullet_points: 1 vCPU Intel Xeon E5-2686 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory with no bundled local storage
Burstable CPU allocation utilizing the Xen hypervisor
Low-tier benchmark performance across CPU and memory tests
Best suited for light static web serving, microservices, and testing
